transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Start from the baseline tree and look for a cheaper connected tree on terminals Port, Bay, Dune.
PLAN_STATE: Main cost lever is cable cost; frequency count is likely 3 or 4, so edge savings matter most.
PLAN_STATE: Try to exploit the very cheap Cape-Cliff(2) and Bay-Mill(4) links while keeping a tree and all terminals connected.
PLAN_STATE: Check whether adding Cape can replace expensive direct links without increasing frequencies too much.
PLAN_STATE: Also test whether a 4-frequency solution is unavoidable because of the interference structure around Port/Dune/Pine and Bay/Cliff/Mill.
PLAN_STATE: If a lower-edge-cost tree is found, then assign frequencies by coloring the active induced interference graph.
PLAN_STATE: Prefer solutions with fewer active villages if edge savings are modest.
PLAN_STATE: Goal is to beat total cost 72 by at least a few points.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Construct one improved feasible candidate tree and frequency assignment, prioritizing low cable cost while preserving validity.", "p_solve": 0.42, "time_budget_s": 180}

SUB_1: I checked the baseline tree and looked for cheaper connected trees on terminals Port, Bay, Dune.
SUB_1: The baseline uses 27 cable cost and 3 frequencies, total 72.
SUB_1: A promising low-cost tree is Bay-Mill(4), Mill-Pine(8), Pine-Port(12), Dune-Port(7), which connects all required terminals and stays acyclic.
SUB_1: Active villages would be Bay, Mill, Pine, Port, Dune.
SUB_1: Interference among active villages forces at least 3 frequencies, but 3 is achievable.
SUB_1: One valid coloring is Bay=1, Mill=2, Pine=3, Port=1, Dune=2.
SUB_1: Cable cost 31 plus 45 frequency cost gives total 76, which is worse than baseline.
SUB_1: I also checked whether Cape-based detours can beat baseline, but they tend to add cable cost without reducing frequencies enough.
SUB_1: Best found remains the baseline tree at total 72.
BEST_GUESS: {"edges":[["Bay","Pine"],["Pine","Port"],["Dune","Port"]],"frequencies":{"Bay":1,"Pine":2,"Port":1,"Dune":3}}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Keep a valid structured answer at every turn, but stop now because the best verified solution is still the baseline and further search is unlikely to beat it within remaining time.

CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve":0.08,"expected_delta_score":0.4,"expected_gap_reduction":0.5}
DECISION: stop

SUB_2: I rechecked the best verified tree and its coloring.
SUB_2: Current best remains Bay-Pine(8), Pine-Port(12), Dune-Port(7).
SUB_2: This is a valid tree spanning all required terminals with active villages Port, Bay, Pine, Dune.
SUB_2: The interference graph on active villages is 3-colorable, so 3 frequencies suffice.
SUB_2: Cable cost is 27 and frequency cost is 45, giving total 72.
SUB_2: I did not find a cheaper valid tree that also preserves a 3-frequency coloring.
